IE.title: LG Water Inlet Error (IE)
IE.summary: The washing machine is unable to fill with water or fill timing thresholds have been exceeded.
causes: Closed or restricted municipal water supply taps
Debris, lime, or silt accumulation inside the inlet mesh filters
Defective electrical inlet solenoid valves
checks: Verify that your external water supply valves are completely open
Unscrew the inlet hose at the back and clean the plastic mesh filter screens safely
repairs: Replacement of the modular water inlet solenoid valve cluster assembly

faq.q: Can low overhead tank pressure cause this code?
faq.a: Yes. If your water pressure is very low, the machine will time out before the tub reaches its target volume.

OE.title: LG Drainage Fault Error (OE)
OE.summary: The appliance has failed to extract or pump out wastewater within the designated 5-minute safety threshold.
causes: Clogged drain pump filter assembly at the bottom front
Blocked, twisted, or kinked external discharge hose pipeline
Burned out or mechanically stuck synchronous drain pump motor
checks: Open the lower emergency panel flap, release the plug, and clean out coins, lint, or pins
Ensure the drainage hose line is not pinched behind the machine frame layout
repairs: Drain pump winding testing and full extraction motor replacement

faq.q: How can I safely empty the machine with water stuck inside?
faq.a: Use the small rubber emergency drain line located next to the main twist filter plug first to drain water into a shallow pan.

UE.title: LG Unbalanced Load Warning (UE)
UE.summary: The control board safely aborted high-speed spinning operations due to an asymmetric mass distribution in the drum.
causes: Washing a single heavy fabric item (e.g., a heavy blanket, jeans, or thick rug)
The washing machine chassis is unlevel or sitting unevenly on the floor profile
Worn out or detached internal suspension rod damper assemblies
checks: Pause the operational cycle, manually open the door, and separate tangled clothes evenly
Physically shake the corners of the chassis to check if the bottom leveling legs are flat
repairs: Threaded leg calibration adjustments or complete structural swap of inner balancing tension springs

faq.q: What is the difference between small 'uE' and capital 'UE'?
faq.a: A lowercase 'uE' means the machine is actively trying to redistribute the clothes. A capital 'UE' means it has failed and stopped for safety.

LE.title: LG Motor Lock Error (LE)
LE.summary: The washing machine motor is locked up or encountering excessive rotational resistance.
causes: Severe internal load weight exceeding structural direct-drive capacities
Foreign material jammed tightly between the stator framework and rotor wheel assembly
Defective or cracked motor hall sensor module failing to log tachometer feedback
checks: Turn off the power, remove some heavy clothing lines, and wait 30 minutes for the thermal trip to auto-reset
With power off, try rotating the inner drum manually to check for absolute mechanical locks
repairs: Replacement of the direct-drive hall sensor array or complete motor stator winding rebuilds

faq.q: Does a burning smell accompany the LE error code layout?
faq.a: If caused by an over-taxed electrical coil, a brief electronic components smell can emit. Unplug the system immediately.

DE.title: LG Door Lock Lockout Error (DE)
DE.summary: The safety control logic is unable to confirm that the main door mechanism is safely latched closed.
causes: The door is not fully pushed against the latch frame
Broken or warped plastic lock striker hook on the door inner lip
Burned out electronic solenoid contacts inside the door interlock safety switch box
checks: Firmly shut the door assembly and ensure no fabric edges are caught inside the rubber seal boundaries
Inspect the strike hook to confirm it cleanly snaps into the entry port slot
repairs: Unseating the rubber door bellow ring to remove and replace the main electronic lock switch component

faq.q: Can I bypass this door lock using wiring jumps?
faq.a: It is not recommended. Bypassing safety lock structures on computerized direct drive configurations risks burning traces off the main motherboard.

FE.title: LG Overfill / Overflow Error (FE)
FE.summary: Water levels inside the drum have exceeded calibrated maximum safety heights.
causes: Faulty water inlet valve failing to close mechanically even when power cuts
Blocked, punctured, or scale-clogged water level pressure sensor tube links
Defective core pressure sensor chip flagging wrong voltage metrics
checks: If water continues pouring in while the machine is completely unplugged, close your primary municipal feed tap manually
Check if excessive soap foam is trickling out, creating a false air lock in the monitoring lines
repairs: Installation change of the faulty direct water valves or replacing the pressure switch sensor component

faq.q: Why does the drain pump run continuously when FE displays?
faq.a: This is a built-in protective safeguard logic. The computer tries to aggressively drain the drum to prevent flooding inside your home layout.

PE.title: LG Water Level Sensor Error (PE)
PE.summary: The electronic pressure sensor module is registering open circuit states or anomalous frequency signals.
causes: Corroded or loose wire connector terminals on the upper pressure sensor module head
Punctured, split, or insect-blocked air tube path leading up from the tub base connection link
Internal failure of the sensor microchip componentry
checks: Unplug the machine, remove the top cover panel, and check the thin translucent rubber air line for localized damage or pinch lines
repairs: Replacing the structural electronic pressure switch module assembly and resetting data connections

TE.title: LG Thermistor / Temperature Sensor Error (TE)
TE.summary: The diagnostic system cannot accurately track water heating configurations due to out-of-bounds component resistance signals.
causes: Defective temperature logging thermistor module clamped onto the heating element frame
Broken or loose electrical wiring strings connecting the sensor back to the main control board
Calcium scale build-up insulated coating over the sensor tip blinding its monitoring parameters
checks: Power off the machine and do a hard system reset by keeping the power isolated for 10 full minutes
repairs: Replacing the low-profile terminal sensor thermistor or repairing damaged wiring harnesses

CE.title: LG Current Overload Error (CE)
CE.summary: An abnormally high electrical current layout draw has been registered by the main motor inverter circuits.
causes: Localized power grid spikes or unstable generator voltage frequencies inside the workspace layout
Shorted electrical winding circuits inside the primary motor stator array
Short-circuit on the IPM inverter module located inside the main control board housing
checks: Unplug the machine line instantly. Ensure your voltage stabilizer or primary supply power matches standard bounds
repairs: Component-level mainboard PCB power repair track tracing or total module substitution fixes

AE.title: LG Water Leak Detection Alarm (AE)
AE.summary: The safety monitoring float switch in the bottom baseline chassis pan has been tripped by accumulated pooling fluids.
causes: Internal tub puncture or cracking on structural plastic tub junctions
Loose internal connection hose clips or split rubber connection couplings
Excessive water overflow from a blocked or leaking detergent dispenser channel
checks: Inspect underneath the bottom trim plate structure to locate external puddles or leaking lines directly
repairs: Locating and replacing the compromised inner rubber distribution tube, gasket seal, or underlying switch assembly

CL.title: LG Child Lock Mode Active (CL)
CL.summary: The systemic button interface protection lock structure is turned on to disable input parameters.
causes: Accidental or intentional user key combination activation matching safety locks
checks: Locate the two buttons bracketed together with a child lock or key graphic indicator on your physical display face layer. Hold them down simultaneously for 3 full seconds to turn it off.

faq.q: Why won't the door open when CL is displayed?
faq.a: CL locks the button dashboard panel. If a cycle is currently running inside, the safety lock will stay engaged until paused or completed.

SUD.title: LG Excessive Foam Safe Lockout (SUD)
SUD.summary: The operational system has registered an excessive build-up of soap suds preventing normal drum cycle rotations.
causes: Using non-front-load rated high-sudsing manual laundry detergents inside automatic drums
Severe detergent dosing quantities exceeding baseline water capabilities
checks: Do not open the door. Allow the machine to stand idle for 30–45 minutes to let the foam liquefy naturally. Then execute a clean rinse-and-spin sequence.

faq.q: Can I use standard hand-wash detergent?
faq.a: No. Always seek out 'HE' (High Efficiency) low-sudsing detergent lines to safeguard electronic monitoring sensors.

4C.title: Samsung Water Supply Error (4C)
4C.summary: The control board has flagged insufficient or restricted water entry volume feeds.
causes: Kinked supply hose piping or closed overhead main roof tank supply links
Debris or sand filling up the micro mesh screens inside the water input threads
Failing magnetic flow solenoid valve gates
checks: Uncouple backend hoses and clean the internal yellow/blue plastic filter plugs safely
Confirm your borehole or municipal pump is turned on and generating steady line speed pressure
repairs: Replacing the dual-chamber configuration computer-controlled water manifold valve unit

faq.q: Is this exactly corresponding to legacy 4E indicators?
faq.a: Yes. Legacy digital Samsung profiles use the '4E' indicator, while newer platform models express this exact fault as '4C'.

5C.title: Samsung Drainage Failure Error (5C)
5C.summary: Core water extraction operations have timed out due to physical path blocks or mechanical system failures.
causes: Pins, coins, buttons, or loose fabrics wedged tight inside the clean-out filter chamber impeller area
Internal drain channel transfer tube links collapsed or crushed
Burned out electronic drain actuator assembly coils
checks: Unscrew the lower right face circular plastic filter plug cap and pull out blockages manually
Ensure the waste pipe connection point height stays below the 1-meter threshold boundary layout
repairs: Complete installation swap out of the low-profile under-chassis electric centrifugal drain pump module

faq.q: My dashboard screen outputs 5E. Is it matching?
faq.a: Yes. '5E' is the legacy system version nomenclature corresponding directly to modern '5C' indicators.

UB.title: Samsung Centrifugal Unbalanced mass warning (UB)
UB.summary: The mechanical drum spin cycle was safely halted due to concentrated off-center load distribution profiles.
causes: Washing heavy absorbent sheets mixed in with lightweight synthetic lace garments
Unlevel locknuts on foundation floor support frame leveling feet
Worn-out or weak mechanical inner damper suspension piston rods
checks: Open the hatch door and redistribute lumped wet items evenly across the stainless steel drum edges
Try to rock the outer chassis structure manually. If it shifts, adjust the leveler legs flat against the floor plan
repairs: Installation overhaul swap of the internal tension dampener suspension assembly kit

faq.q: Does this fault affect both top-load and front-load setups?
faq.a: Yes. Both design archetypes monitor motor feedback variations and display 'Ub' or 'UE' when rotational balance limits are exceeded.

DE.title: Samsung Door Closing Safety Fault (DE)
DE.summary: The control logic cannot pass safety checks because the door status module reports an open state circuit.
causes: Physical debris obstruction caught inside the door strike capture channel entry port
Faulty electronic microswitch contacts inside the safety door lock block module
Broken or loose connection cables between the door frame and the main circuit board
checks: Examine the metal or plastic strike pin on the door hinge panel for structural fractures, then firmly pull the door shut
repairs: Disassembly of the front outer wire ring retainer to install a new door safety interlock lock unit

HE.title: Samsung Water Heating Failure Error (HE)
HE.summary: An out-of-bounds water heating rate parameter has been flagged by the main computer control array.
causes: Open circuit state or short circuit inside the core internal immersion heater coil elements
Severe scale insulation build-up around the heater shaft causing localized thermal spikes
Defective control relay components on the main electronic motherboard module
checks: Unplug the system line and verify your power socket lines do not exhibit signs of burning or melting
repairs: Full installation replacement of the high-amperage internal immersion element heating unit

faq.q: Can I use cold cycles if HE stays active?
faq.a: On most Samsung firmware revisions, an active HE flag locks out all cycles for safety, including cold cycles, until cleared.

LE.title: Samsung Fluid Leak / Level Reduction Error (LE)
LE.summary: The sensor arrays have logged an abnormal drop in water level during active cycles, or a baseline water leak has occurred.
causes: The drainage hose line has fallen flat on the ground plane, causing an unintentional gravity siphoning effect
Punctured tub connection links, cracks, or split front door rubber gaskets
Internal bottom base plate leak float detection safety trip
checks: Ensure the rear drain hose loops upward at least 60–90 cm before diving down into your external plumbing line
repairs: Re-seating drain pipe channels, replacing split perimeter gaskets, or tracking and sealing cracked tub points

3E.title: Samsung Motor Drive Failure Error (3E)
3E.summary: The main electronic drive tracking system has lost normal contact with the motor array or logged rotation errors.
causes: Worn down carbon contact brushes inside older universal motor configurations
Open circuits or dislodged terminal blocks inside the primary drive harness strings
Blown power IGBT switching chips on the main digital motherboard assembly
checks: Turn off the power line, wait 5 minutes, and then restart to check if the controller logic can reset its communication pathways
repairs: Installation changes targeting motor brush elements or technical repair processing on the primary PCB circuit traces

SUD.title: Samsung Suds Lock Safety Detection (SUD)
SUD.summary: Excessive soap foam has filled the drum cavity, stalling normal cycle operation parameters.
causes: Over-dosing with standard laundry detergents or using non-front-load rated washing soaps
checks: Allow the machine to sit idle. The internal logic will run an eco-foam breakdown routine or wait for the suds to settle naturally before unlocking.

NF.title: Samsung No Fill Timed Out Alert (NF)
NF.summary: The system has failed to confirm water entry matching low-level baseline steps within its safety timelines.
causes: Closed external link valves or a completely dried out localized storage tank framework
Severely restricted or jammed water inlet valves
checks: Verify that water pressure is flowing steadily from nearby domestic taps to confirm general utility availability
repairs: Replacing failing magnetic direct control fluid intake valve module sets

UC.title: Samsung Volumetric Power Unstable Warning (UC)
UC.summary: The main onboard computer has detected dangerous electrical voltage fluctuations from your power source.
causes: Unstable local generator output or a major grid sag/surge under load configurations
Failing power control lines or filtering circuits on the mainboard module
checks: Immediately switch off the machine line. Do not operate your washer on low-power public power links or unrated local generator sets.
repairs: Installation integration of an external digital automatic voltage protector/stabilizer unit or primary PCB repair

faq.q: What kVA generator is recommended safely?
faq.a: An automatic front-load washer with heating configurations requires a minimum of a robust 3.5 kVA to 5.5 kVA pure sine-wave generator to handle startup and heating current draws safely.

F1E1.title: Whirlpool Main Control Board Fault (F1E1)
F1E1.summary: The primary electronic control processing unit has encountered an internal hardware or EEPROM firmware validation error.
causes: Electrical grid power surge damage tracing through primary transformer filter pathways
Corrupted central processing memory sectors on the logic board
checks: Disconnect the main electrical line plug for 15 minutes, re-insert, and wait to see if the internal microprocessor boots up normally
repairs: Professional technical component tracing and chip rebuilds on the central motherboard or total unit replacement

F2.title: Whirlpool Drainage Timeout Fault (F2)
F2.summary: The central controller has flagged an inability to extract water volumes past baseline cycle limits.
causes: Trapped fibers, strings, or stray loose materials sealing up the impeller fins
Collapsed underpan flexible extraction pipeline structures
checks: Inspect external exhaust pipelines for structural kinks or flattenings restricting free water flow trajectories
repairs: Full installation substitution of the integrated layout magnetic synchronous drainage motor block assembly

F5E1.title: Whirlpool Door Interlock Safety Fault (F5E1)
F5E1.summary: The lock mechanism configuration indicates an unlatched state preventing cycle initiation routines.
causes: Warped cabinet face frame alignment preventing clean mechanical strike interaction
Internal electrical contact burns on the latch switch solenoid terminals
checks: Verify that the physical door fits squarely with the primary cabinet profile and press firmly until a clicking lock sound occurs
repairs: Removal and replacement assembly targeting the internal safety lock toggle box system

F6.title: Whirlpool Motor Drive Error (F6)
F6.summary: The main processing module cannot communicate with the motor tachometer generator or inverter module blocks.
causes: Severely worn down mechanical carbon terminal brushes inside the motor frame layout
Broken or loose connection line pin plugs on the lower drive harness loops
checks: Isolate electrical lines and visually inspect beneath the motor base for loose or dragging connection wires
repairs: Refitting carbon motor contact inserts or a full swap replacement targeting the electric drive assembly unit

F8E1.title: Whirlpool Long Fill Water Fault (F8E1)
F8E1.summary: The appliance water fill rate is too slow, causing an automatic cycle timeout to protect internal heater components.
causes: Low pressure overhead feed lines or blocked rear connector filter caps
Defective intake electrical solenoid assembly valve mechanics
checks: Disconnect rear hoses to clean the integrated stainless internal screen structures directly
repairs: Installation change replacement targeting the standard modular inlet line distributor valves
